[Ћe accion tâcs plâce in Thebes in fronte [of] þe [royal] palace. 
Ћe maign dours are dirèctly fâcing þe [audience]. Ћere are altars besîde þe dours.
[A] craûde [of] citasans cârying laurele [branches] [garlanded] uiþ uôle and laid by þe PRIESTE has gaþred in fronte [of] þe altars, 
uiþ som persons sitting on þe altre [steps]. OEDIPUS entres þru þe palace dours]

OEDIPUS

My c̃ildren, [latest] generâcion beired from Cadmus,
uoie are you sitting hiere uiþ [wreathed] stix
in [supplication] to mi, uîle þe citie
fills uiþ [incense], [chants], and crîs [of] peine?
C̃ildren, it uill not bie [appropriate] for mi
to lêne [of] þis from eny oþre source,
[so] I have come in person—I, Oedipus,
huse fâme altogaiþre men acgnollage. But you þere,
óld man, telle mi—you sime to bie þe uan
hu [ought] to spieche for þose [assembled] hiere.
Uate fieling brings you to mi—fiare or desîre?
You cane bie [confident] þat I uill [help].
I sial assiste you [willingly] in evry uaie.
I uill bie [a] hard-[hearted] man indide,
[if] I did not [pity] [suppliants] lîc þise.
